perturbation.original_text: CAPSUGEL shall notify CARDAX immediately, and in no event, no later than three (3) business day(s), after it receives any contact or communication from any governmental or regulatory authority, including without limitation the FDA, that in any way relates to or may have an impact on a Product or the CAPSUGEL Development Activities.
perturbation.changed_text: CAPSUGEL shall notify CARDAX within ten (10) business days after it receives any contact or communication from any governmental or regulatory authority, including without limitation the FDA, that in any way relates to or may have an impact on a Product or the CAPSUGEL Development Activities.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the notification period from 3 business days to 10 business days for regulatory contact may cause non-compliance with cGMP regulations, which emphasizes prompt reporting. Delaying notification could impede timely corrective actions.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 211.22 mandates written procedures for responsibilities and controls applicable to the quality control unit, which includes reporting and investigation of any issue having an impact on drug safety, identity, strength, quality, and purity of drug products. Changing the timeline could impede prompt reporting and investigation of potential safety issues which might render the firm in non-compliance with this section.

perturbation.justification: The modified text extends the notification period from 3 to 10 business days. The Law Explanation and Perturbation Explanation suggest this delay conflicts with 21 CFR § 211.22, which mandates prompt reporting and investigation of issues impacting drug safety. While the provided snippets do not contain the full text of 21 CFR § 211.22, the Law Explanation indicates that changing the timeline could impede prompt reporting and investigation of potential safety issues, leading to non-compliance.

perturbation.original_text: CAPSUGEL agrees to retain all such Records for a period of five (5) years after the expiration of the Term or after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: CAPSUGEL agrees to retain all such Records for a period of two (2) years after the expiration of the Term or after termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the record retention period from 5 years to 2 years for manufacturing records is inconsistent with FDA requirements for drug products.
perturbation.law_explanation: 21 CFR § 211.180(a) stipulates that records required by this regulation shall be retained for at least one year after the expiration date of the batch or, for certain records, a longer period. Reducing the retention period to two years may not align with the one-year-after-expiration requirement and subsequent analysis, if required.

perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the record retention period from 5 years to 2 years, while the Law Explanation states that 21 CFR § 211.180(a) requires records to be retained for at least one year after the expiration date of the batch, potentially longer for certain records. Although the scraped snippets do not provide the exact text of 21 CFR § 211.180(a), the Perturbation Explanation indicates that this change is inconsistent with FDA requirements, which reinforces that reducing the retention period is a violation.
